title: chromolaena subscandens
intro: Chromolaena subscandens, also known as the 'Wild Tobacco' or 'Eupatorium', is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is native to tropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Chromolaena subscandens include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antiseptic, and help in the treatment of respiratory issues. Chromolaena subscandens can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or used as a tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice to treat skin conditions.
